message:
fix(tests): mark slow two-tier bridge tests as ignored

The thread-safety tests for TwoTierBridge require ML embedder
initialization which can take 60+ seconds. Mark them as #[ignore]
so they don't block CI runs. They can still be run explicitly
with `cargo test -- --ignored`.

Tests affected:
- get_or_init_two_tier_bridge_is_thread_safe (10 threads)
- get_or_init_two_tier_bridge_stress_100_threads (100 threads)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -504,9 +504,9 @@
 {"id":"br-2bbt.18.1","title":"Add FieldScope facet (Subject/Body/Both) to existing FacetRail","description":"CRITICAL CONTEXT: The Search screen ALREADY has 5 facets including SortOrder (NewestFirst/
OldestFirst/Relevance) and Scope (Global/Project/Product). This bead adds a NEW FieldScope
facet for search-field filtering, NOT project-scope or sort (which already exist).

## Task: Add FieldScope Facet to Existing FacetRail

1. Add FieldScope enum to search.rs:
   enum FieldScope { SubjectAndBody, SubjectOnly, BodyOnly }
   impl FieldScope { fn next(&self) -> Self { ... } }  // cycle like existing facets

2. Add to FacetSlot enum (search.rs, line ~499):
   FacetSlot::FieldScope  // new variant

3. Add field to SearchCockpitScreen (line ~539):
   field_scope: FieldScope  // default: SubjectAndBody

4. Add toggle case to toggle_active_facet() (line ~893):
   FacetSlot::FieldScope => self.field_scope = self.field_scope.next()

5. Add rendering in FacetRail draw section (line ~1828):
   Render as: "Field: Subject+Body" or "Field: Subject" or "Field: Body"
   Use same FACET_ACTIVE_FG/FACET_LABEL_FG styling as existing facets

6. Modify FTS query construction (wherever search query is built):
   - SubjectAndBody: standard MATCH query (current behavior, no change)
   - SubjectOnly: use FTS5 column filter 'subject:term' or WHERE subject LIKE '%term%'
   - BodyOnly: use FTS5 column filter 'body_md:term' or WHERE body_md LIKE '%term%'

NOTE: Do NOT modify or duplicate the existing SortOrder or Scope facets.

## Tests
- Unit: FieldScope.next() cycles correctly (SubjectAndBody -> SubjectOnly -> BodyOnly -> SubjectAndBody)
- Unit: SubjectOnly scope produces FTS5 column filter in query
- Unit: BodyOnly scope produces FTS5 column filter in query
- Unit: SubjectAndBody produces standard MATCH (regression test)
- Unit: existing 5 facets still work after adding 6th (no regressions)
- Snapshot: FacetRail rendering with 6 facets (verify all visible)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.480650676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:24:05.694160040Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:24:05.694141566Z","close_reason":"Implemented FieldScope facet with full cycling support, FTS5 column filtering, and 8 unit tests. All 72 search tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filters","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.18.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.480650676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.18.2","title":"Add hit highlighting and snippet previews to search results","description":"Enhance search result display with hit highlighting and snippets:

1. For each search result, generate a snippet:
   - Extract 200 chars around the first match in body_md
   - If match is in subject, show full subject + first 100 chars of body
   - Truncate with ellipsis at word boundaries

2. Hit highlighting:
   - Bold/inverted the matched search terms within the snippet
   - Use theme-aware highlight color (search_highlight semantic color)
   - Highlight in both subject and snippet

3. Match count badge:
   - Show total result count next to Search Results header
   - Format: Search Results (42 matches)

4. Result entry layout per row:
   - Line 1: [Subject] (bold) | [Sender] | [Date]
   - Line 2: [Snippet with highlights] (dim text, highlighted matches)
   - Line 3: [Thread: thread_id badge] | [Project: slug badge] (if global mode)

## Tests
- Unit: snippet extraction with match at start, middle, end of body
- Unit: snippet truncation at word boundaries
- Unit: highlight positions map correctly to rendered text
- Snapshot: search result with highlighted terms
- E2E: search for known term, verify highlighted in result","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.677871534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:40:58.107847267Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:40:58.107778288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlighting","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.18.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.677871534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.18.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.18.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:20.061857237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":348,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.18.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation note: When building hit highlighting, design it behind a HighlightProvider trait so the highlighting backend can be swapped from FTS5 SNIPPET() to Tantivy highlight API when Search V3 (br-2tnl.3.4) lands. This avoids a rewrite and allows parallel development. The trait should take (query_terms: Vec<String>, text: String) and return Vec<HighlightSpan> with start/end byte offsets.","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:40:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.18.3","title":"Add query syntax help tooltip to search bar","description":"Show query syntax help when ? is pressed while search bar (QueryBar) has focus:

IMPORTANT KEYBINDING CONTEXT:
- ? is the GLOBAL help overlay toggle (defined in tui_app.rs)
- When QueryBar has focus, key events go to the TextInput widget FIRST
- The ? key must be INTERCEPTED by the Search screen's QueryBar handler
  BEFORE it bubbles up to the global handler
- When QueryBar does NOT have focus (e.g., on FacetRail or ResultList),
  ? continues to toggle the global help overlay as usual

1. Help popup (small overlay near search bar) shows:
   - AND/OR: combine terms (e.g., error AND deploy)
   - Quotes: exact phrase (e.g., "build failed")
   - Prefix: wildcard matching (e.g., deploy*)
   - NOT: exclude terms (e.g., error NOT test)
   - Column: filter by field (e.g., subject:deploy)

2. Help popup dismissed on any key press (or Esc)
3. Help popup styled as bordered box with dim background
4. If user types ? while composing a query, the ? char is NOT inserted into the search text
   (it's consumed by the help popup trigger)

## Alternative: Use Ctrl+? or F1 instead
If intercepting ? causes confusion (users might want literal ? in queries), consider
using F1 or Ctrl+/ instead. F1 is conventional for help and doesn't conflict with anything.

## Tests
- Snapshot: help popup rendering near search bar
- Unit: ? key triggers help popup ONLY when QueryBar has focus
- Unit: ? key still triggers global help when FacetRail/ResultList has focus
- Unit: any key dismisses the help popup","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:53.198961789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:12:53.186134019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:12:53.186110845Z","close_reason":"Implemented query-bar syntax help popup with key interception and tests; full test gate blocked by unrelated existing compile errors in threads.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["help","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.18.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:53.198961789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2bbt.19","title":"[track] Thread Conversation View with Expandable Messages","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/{project_slug}/thread/{thread_id} shows a Gmail-style conversation:
- Expandable message cards (click to expand/collapse)
- Last message expanded by default, others collapsed with preview
- Avatar with first-letter initials
- Thread metadata (ID, message count, participants list)
- Importance badges on individual messages
- Message ID display
- Sender names and timestamps

The TUI Threads screen (#3) shows a thread list and message detail, but messages within a thread are shown in a flat table, not as a conversation. The thread reading experience should feel like reading an email thread.

## Implementation Approach

1. Enhance the Threads screen detail pane:
   - When viewing a thread, show messages as stacked cards (not a table)
   - Each card has: sender initial badge | sender name | timestamp | importance badge
   - Collapsed card: single line with sender + subject preview (first 80 chars)
   - Expanded card: full message body with markdown rendering
   - Most recent message expanded by default

2. Card interaction:
   - Enter on collapsed card expands it (and collapses previously expanded)
   - Or: toggle mode where multiple can be expanded simultaneously
   - e key: expand all, c key: collapse all
   - Space: toggle current card expansion

3. Thread metadata header:
   - Thread ID, message count, participant list
   - Time span: first message date -> last message date
   - Unread count indicator

4. Participant summary:
   - Show unique senders with message counts
   - Color-coded sender names (consistent color per agent name)
   - Click/Enter on sender -> deep link to agent profile

5. Rendering:
   - Use Block widget for each card border
   - Expanded card uses markdown renderer for body
   - Card separator: thin horizontal rule between cards
   - Scroll entire thread if it exceeds screen height

## Acceptance Criteria
- Thread messages render as expandable cards (not flat table)
- Last message expanded by default, others collapsed
- Enter/Space toggles card expansion
- e/c expand/collapse all
- Thread metadata header shows participants, count, time span
- Collapsed cards show sender + preview snippet
- Expanded cards render markdown body
- Consistent sender color coding
- Unit tests for card state management, expansion logic
- Snapshot tests for collapsed and expanded card rendering
- E2E test: create thread with 5 messages, verify conversation view, expand/collapse","notes":"## Large Thread Handling

For threads with more than 20 messages, the conversation view should NOT render all cards at once. Instead:

1. Show the most recent 20 messages as expandable cards
2. At the top, show a Load older messages button with count: Show 15 more messages
3. Loading older messages prepends cards to the list (maintains scroll position)
4. This avoids rendering 50+ cards simultaneously which would degrade performance

The 20-message threshold is configurable via AM_TUI_THREAD_PAGE_SIZE env var (default: 20).

## Consistent Sender Coloring

Agent name colors should be deterministic and consistent across all screens:
- Hash agent name string to a color index
- Use 8 distinct theme-aware colors from the current theme palette
- Same agent always gets same color regardless of screen context
- Colors chosen to be distinguishable even in high-contrast mode","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:30.287145328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:07:28.924464240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conversation","threads","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.19","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:30.287145328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- E2E test: create thread with 5 messages, verify conversation view, expand/collapse","notes":"## Large Thread Handling

The 20-message threshold is configurable via AM_TUI_THREAD_PAGE_SIZE env var (default: 20).

## Consistent Sender Coloring

Agent name colors should be deterministic and consistent across all screens:
- Hash agent name string to a color index
- Use 8 distinct theme-aware colors from the current theme palette
- Same agent always gets same color regardless of screen context
- Colors chosen to be distinguishable even in high-contrast mode","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:30.287145328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:42.241830753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:42.241809733Z","close_reason":"Track complete: expandable conversation cards and metadata/pagination child tasks are closed (br-2bbt.19.1 + br-2bbt.19.2), with threads screen validation passing (63 targeted tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conversation","threads","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.19","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:30.287145328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.19.1","title":"Implement expandable message card widget for thread view","description":"Create a MessageCard widget for rendering individual messages in thread conversation view:

## File Location Guidance
Add MessageCard to tui_widgets.rs (alongside existing BrailleActivity, MetricTile,
Leaderboard, AnomalyCard, AgentHeatmap). The widget is reusable across thread view
AND potentially search result previews, so it belongs with other shared widgets rather
than being embedded in tui_screens/threads.rs.

1. MessageCard states: Collapsed and Expanded
2. Collapsed rendering (single row, ~2 lines):
   - Left: colored sender initial badge (deterministic color from name hash)
   - Center: sender name (bold) | timestamp (dim) | importance badge (if urgent/high)
   - Below: preview snippet (first 80 chars of body, truncated at word boundary, dim)

3. Expanded rendering (variable height):
   - Header: sender initial badge | sender name | timestamp | importance badge | message ID (dim)
   - Separator: thin horizontal rule
   - Body: full message rendered via ftui_extras::markdown::MarkdownRenderer
   - Footer: [View Full] [Jump to Sender] action hints (dim)

4. Card border: Block widget with rounded corners, theme-aware border color
   - Separator between cards: 1-line gap

5. State management:
   - Most recent message expanded by default
   - Enter/Space toggles current card expansion
   - e expands all, c collapses all
   - Arrow keys navigate between cards

## Tests
- Unit: collapsed card truncates body at 80 chars at word boundary
- Unit: sender color hash is deterministic (same name = same color)
- Unit: sender color hash produces 8 distinct colors for 8 names
- Snapshot: collapsed card rendering
- Snapshot: expanded card with markdown body
- Snapshot: 3 cards (2 collapsed, 1 expanded) stacked","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:20.254513622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:50:15.222695071Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:50:15.222674603Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["threads","tui","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.19.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:20.254513622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2bbt.19.2","title":"Implement thread metadata header and pagination for large threads","description":"Add thread context information and pagination to the conversation view:

1. Thread metadata header (rendered above message cards):
   - Thread ID (styled as badge)
   - Message count: 12 messages
   - Participants: comma-separated list of unique sender names (colored)
   - Time span: first message date ... last message date
   - Unread count: 3 unread (if applicable)

2. Pagination for large threads (>20 messages):
   - Show most recent 20 messages
   - At top: [Load 15 older messages] button
   - Loading appends older cards above current cards
   - Scroll position preserved during load (no jump)
   - Page size configurable via AM_TUI_THREAD_PAGE_SIZE (default: 20)

3. Scroll behavior:
   - If all cards fit in view, no scrollbar
   - If overflow, show scrollbar on right edge
   - PgUp/PgDn scrolls by viewport height
   - Home jumps to oldest visible message, End to newest

## Tests
- Unit: metadata header shows correct participant count
- Unit: pagination loads correct offset of messages
- Unit: page size from env var overrides default
- Snapshot: metadata header with 3 participants, 12 messages
- Snapshot: load-more button at top of thread
- E2E: create thread with 25 messages, verify only 20 shown, load older, verify 25 shown","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:20.446681513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:19:40.166748712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pagination","threads","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.19.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:20.446681513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":408,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.19.2","author":"CopperFalcon","text":"Claiming implementation pass for this bead in current session. Scope: verify current thread metadata/pagination behavior, close gaps, and add/adjust tests for participant count + pagination + env page-size override. Will report verification commands and results here.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:14:12Z"},{"id":409,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.19.2","author":"CopperFalcon","text":"Progress update: added targeted thread-view coverage in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/threads.rs for remaining acceptance checks. New tests: (1) metadata_header_shows_participant_count_and_names, (2) paginated_fetch_respects_offset_for_older_messages, (3) parse_thread_page_size_honors_valid_override, (4) parse_thread_page_size_falls_back_to_default. Also added parse_thread_page_size helper to harden env parsing (trim + positive integer guard). Verification: cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server threads:: --lib -- --nocapture => PASS (63 tests, includes new coverage). cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib => PASS with pre-existing warnings outside this bead. cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib --tests -- -D warnings currently fails on extensive pre-existing lint/API issues in other modules (dashboard, tui_app, search, messages, reservations, timeline, etc.), not introduced by this change set.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:19:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-2bbt.2","title":"[track] Toast Notification System for Real-Time Events","description":"## Rationale

Currently operators must be on the right screen to notice events. A message from OrangeFinch
is invisible if youre viewing Reservations. Toast notifications solve this.

## EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE (VERIFIED FROM CODE — ALL FUNCTIONAL)

1. toast_for_event() EXISTS (tui_app.rs lines 1404-1440):
   Already maps 5 event types to toasts: MessageSent, AgentRegistered, HttpRequest(5xx),
   ServerShutdown, ServerStarted. All others return None.

2. Tick loop ALREADY processes events (lines 769-778):
   Calls events_since(last_toast_seq), maps each via toast_for_event(), pushes to queue.

3. NotificationStack ALREADY renders at z-layer 4 (lines 981-983):
   Positioned, styled, stacked — all working.

4. NotificationQueue initialized with QueueConfig::default() (line 138):
   max_visible=3, max_queued=10, default_duration=5s, position=TopRight

## What This Track Enhances

The toast PIPELINE is complete. This track ENHANCES it with:

1. Additional event mappings (br-2bbt.2.1):
   - MessageReceived (most important missing mapping!)
   - ToolCallEnd (slow execution >5s, error in result_preview)
   - ReservationGranted (exclusive only, low priority)

2. Reservation expiry warnings (br-2bbt.2.1):
   - DERIVED from ReservationGranted TTL tracking in tick loop
   - No ReservationExpiringSoon event exists — must compute in MailAppModel

3. Toast actions (br-2bbt.2.1):
   - Navigate to relevant screen when toast is activated

4. Toast rendering enhancement (br-2bbt.2.2):
   - Audit current NotificationStack quality
   - Add focus behavior (Ctrl+T) for toast interaction

5. Configuration (br-2bbt.2.3):
   - Severity threshold filtering
   - Position, duration, muting env vars

## Acceptance Criteria
- MessageReceived generates toast
- Slow/error tool calls generate warning/error toasts
- Reservation expiry warning fires within 5 minutes of TTL expiration
- Existing 5 toast mappings unchanged (regression)
- Configuration via env vars
- Unit tests for all new mappings and derived events","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:48:50.418848204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:49.202938878Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:49.202907940Z","close_reason":"All child beads complete: br-2bbt.2.1 (event mappings, expiry tracking, actions), br-2bbt.2.2 (toast rendering enhancements with severity colors), br-2bbt.2.3 (config env vars and Ctrl+T focus). Implementation verified in tui_app.rs with full test coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","notifications","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:48:50.418848204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.2.1","title":"Enhance existing toast_for_event() with new mappings, actions, and expiry tracking","description":"CRITICAL CONTEXT: toast_for_event() ALREADY EXISTS (tui_app.rs line 1404-1440) with 5 mappings:

EXISTING MAPPINGS (working today):
1. MessageSent { from, to } -> Info toast: "{from} → {recipients}" (4s)
2. AgentRegistered { name, program } -> Success toast: "{name} ({program})" (4s)
3. HttpRequest { status >= 500, path } -> Error toast: "HTTP {status} on {path}" (6s)
4. ServerShutdown -> Warning toast: "Server shutting down" (8s)
5. ServerStarted { endpoint } -> Success toast: "Server started at {endpoint}" (5s)
6. Everything else -> None

EXISTING TICK LOOP (lines 769-778):
  let new_events = self.state.events_since(self.last_toast_seq);
  for event in &new_events {
      self.last_toast_seq = event.seq().max(self.last_toast_seq);
      if let Some(toast) = toast_for_event(event) {
          self.notifications.notify(toast);
      }
  }

EXISTING RENDERING (lines 981-983):
  NotificationStack::new(&self.notifications).margin(1).render(area, frame);

## Task: ENHANCE Existing Event-to-Toast Mapping

The pipeline EXISTS and WORKS. This bead enhances it with additional mappings, toast actions,
reservation expiry tracking, and configurable severity.

### New Mappings to Add (in toast_for_event):

a. MessageReceived { from, subject, .. } ->
   Info toast: "{from}: {subject}" (truncated to 40 chars), 5s
   NOTE: Only MessageSent currently generates a toast. MessageReceived is arguably MORE
   important since the TUI operator cares about incoming messages, not outgoing ones.
   Consider: replace MessageSent mapping with MessageReceived, or keep both.

b. ToolCallEnd { tool_name, duration_ms, .. } where duration_ms > 5000 ->
   Warning toast: "{tool_name}: {duration_ms}ms" (8s)
   Currently ToolCallEnd generates NO toast.

c. ToolCallEnd { tool_name, result_preview: Some(preview), .. } where preview contains "error"/"Error" ->
   Error toast: "{tool_name} error" (15s)

d. ReservationGranted { agent, paths, exclusive: true, .. } ->
   Info toast: "{agent} locked {paths[0]}" (4s)
   Low priority — informational only.

### New Feature: Reservation Expiry Warning (DERIVED event)

No ReservationExpiringSoon event exists. Must derive from tracking:
- Add field: reservation_tracker: HashMap<String, (String, i64)> to MailAppModel
  Key: "{project}:{agent}:{path}", Value: (display_label, expiry_timestamp_micros)
- On ReservationGranted: insert with expiry = now_micros + ttl_s * 1_000_000
- On ReservationReleased: remove matching entries
- In tick loop (after toast_for_event processing): check for entries expiring within 5 min
  Push Warning toast once per reservation (track warned set to prevent duplicates)

### New Feature: Toast Actions

Each toast currently has no action. Enhance with navigation targets:
- MessageReceived/Sent -> action: navigate to Messages screen
- ReservationGranted -> action: navigate to Reservations screen
- HttpRequest 5xx -> action: navigate to SystemHealth screen
- ToolCallEnd error/slow -> action: navigate to ToolMetrics screen

Implementation: Toast may need an arbitrary data field or callback. Check frankentui Toast
struct for action/data fields. If none, store action map separately in MailAppModel:
  toast_actions: HashMap<ToastId, DeepLink>

### New Feature: Configurable Severity Threshold

- AM_TUI_TOAST_SEVERITY env var (default: info, options: off/error/warning/info)
- In toast_for_event: check threshold before returning Some(toast)
- info: all toasts, warning: only Warning+Error, error: only Error, off: none

## Tests
- Unit: MessageReceived generates Info toast with sender + subject
- Unit: ToolCallEnd with duration > 5000ms generates Warning toast
- Unit: ToolCallEnd with error in preview generates Error toast
- Unit: ToolCallEnd with normal result generates no toast (regression)
- Unit: reservation expiry tracker insert/remove lifecycle
- Unit: expiry warning fires within 5 min, dedup prevents repeat warnings
- Unit: severity threshold filters correctly (set to "error", Info toast filtered out)
- Unit: existing 5 mappings unchanged (regression tests)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:50:40.685233467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:56:11.864018957Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:56:11.863983972Z","close_reason":"Implemented: 4 new event-to-toast mappings (MessageReceived, slow ToolCallEnd, error ToolCallEnd, exclusive ReservationGranted), reservation expiry tracking with 5-min warning window + dedup, configurable AM_TUI_TOAST_SEVERITY env var (off/error/warning/info), severity filtering. 24 new unit tests, all 101 tui_app tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["notifications","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.2.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:50:40.685233467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.2.2","title":"Audit and enhance existing toast overlay rendering","description":"CRITICAL CONTEXT: The view function ALREADY renders notifications:
  NotificationStack::new(&self.notifications).margin(1).render(area, frame)
at z-layer 4. This IS functional rendering. The question is whether it needs enhancement.

## Task: Audit and Enhance Toast Rendering

1. First, verify current NotificationStack rendering quality:
   - Does it show at top-right? (QueueConfig defaults to TopRight)
   - Does it stack multiple toasts? (max_visible defaults to 3)
   - Does it show severity styling? (Toast has ToastStyle enum: Success/Error/Warning/Info)
   - Does it show countdown indicator?
   - Does it handle terminal resize?
   If all of these work already, this bead may require minimal changes.

2. Enhancement targets (only if current rendering is insufficient):
   - Severity-colored left border (if not already styled by frankentui)
   - Countdown timer text in toast (e.g., "3s" remaining)
   - Toast width: min(40, terminal_width/3) — verify frankentui handles this

3. Toast z-ordering (ALREADY CORRECT):
   - Layer 4: toasts (NotificationStack)
   - Layer 5: command palette
   - Layer 6: help overlay
   DO NOT change this — it's already right.

4. Focused toast stack rendering:
   - When Ctrl+T focuses toast stack, highlighted border on selected toast
   - This is NEW behavior — NotificationStack may not support focus highlighting natively
   - May need custom rendering or wrapper around NotificationStack
   - When focused: show action hint text per toast (e.g., "Enter: Jump to messages")

## frankentui Toast API (verified):
- Toast::new(content).icon(ToastIcon).style(ToastStyle).duration(Duration)
- ToastPosition: TopLeft/TopCenter/TopRight/BottomLeft/BottomCenter/BottomRight
- ToastAnimationPhase: Entering/Visible/Exiting/Hidden
- Entrance: SlideFromTop/SlideFromRight/FadeIn/None
- Exit: FadeOut/SlideOut/None

## Tests
- Snapshot: verify current NotificationStack rendering is acceptable (may already pass)
- Snapshot: 3 stacked toasts with different ToastStyles (Info/Warning/Error)
- Snapshot: focused toast stack with selected toast highlighted border
- Unit: toast position stays within terminal bounds on resize","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:50:40.854550081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:32:14.321695388Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:32:14.321623904Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","notifications","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:50:40.854550081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:08.965375433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2e9jp.4","title":"Phase 4: WASM Web Mode - Browser-Based Agent Mail Dashboard","description":"# Phase 4: WASM Web Mode - Browser-Based Agent Mail Dashboard

## Objective
Compile the TUI to WebAssembly for browser-based access to Agent Mail state.

## Architecture

Browser (WASM TUI with Canvas Terminal via frankenterm-web) <-> WebSocket <-> MCP Agent Mail Server (State Sync Service with delta compression)

## Key Deliverables

- T4.1: WASM Build Target - Configure Cargo for wasm32-unknown-unknown
- T4.2: Web Frontend Shell - HTML/JS shell with Canvas terminal
- T4.3: WebSocket State Sync - Delta-compressed real-time updates
- T4.4: Cloudflare Pages Deployment - Static hosting with global CDN

## Benefits

1. Remote monitoring: View agent mail state from any device
2. No installation: Just open a URL
3. Share state: Send links to specific views
4. Mobile access: Works on tablets/phones

## Performance Targets

- WASM bundle size: <2MB gzipped
- Initial load time: <3s on 3G
- Frame time: <33ms (30 FPS)
- WebSocket latency: <100ms

## Dependencies
Requires Phase 3 (Visual Polish) completion. Theme system must work in browser.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:54:47.897453585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:15:13.584670613Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2e9jp.4","depends_on_id":"br-2e9jp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:54:47.897453585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2e9jp.4","depends_on_id":"br-2e9jp.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:55:12.582774921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2e9jp.5","title":"Phase 5: Testing & Documentation","description":"# Phase 5: Testing & Documentation

## Objective
Comprehensive test coverage and operator documentation for the revamped TUI.

## Key Deliverables

- T5.1: Unit Tests - Tests for all new TUI components (ScreenManager, StateBridge, widgets)
- T5.2: E2E PTY Tests - PTY-based integration tests simulating real terminal sessions
- T5.3: Golden Snapshots - Visual regression tests using insta
- T5.4: Operator Documentation - Configuration guide, troubleshooting, best practices
- T5.5: Performance Regression Tests - Benchmark suite to catch regressions
- T5.6: Accessibility E2E Tests - Automated accessibility checks for all screens
- T5.7: WASM-Specific Tests - Browser-based tests for WASM build

## Coverage Targets

- ScreenManager: 95%
- StateBridge: 90%
- VirtualizedList: 95%
- CommandPalette: 90%
- Theme system: 85%
- WASM bindings: 80%

## Test Infrastructure

CI Pipeline:
- cargo test --all-features
- cargo test --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
- ./scripts/e2e_pty_tests.sh
- cargo bench --no-run (ensure benchmarks compile)

## Dependencies
Requires all implementation phases complete. Can run in parallel with Phase 4 (WASM).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:56:44.030888226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:15:23.842827173Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2e9jp.5","depends_on_id":"br-2e9jp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:56:44.030888226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2e9jp.5","depends_on_id":"br-2e9jp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:57:14.428568687Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
